title: Extraction of High Molecular Weight DNA from Aphids for Long-Read Sequencing
author: Roland Wouters
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bhftj3nn
materials: (Optional) repeat the wash step 2 more times.

Assess the quality of DNA by electrophoresis and spectrophotometry. A single, high-molecular-weight band should be visible, a smear at lower molecular weight indicates fragmentation and/or RNA contamination. Absorbance ratio of 260:280 and 260:230 should both be 1.8 or greater. If you suspended the pellet in TE, ensure than you blank the spectrophotometer with TE. Determine the concentration of the DNA using a fluorescent dye-binding based method such as the Qubit (Invitrogen).

steps.title: Grind approx
steps.instruction: Grind approx. 10mg (20 frozen aphids; originated from a single female, one froghopper) in a 1.5ml Eppendorf tube, with a plastic pestle, pre-cooled in liquid nitrogen. Ensure that tissue remains frozen throughout. When grinding the samples do not turn the pestle, but crush the aphids via an up-down movement.

steps.title: Add 600 µl of reagent 1, with RNAse added
steps.instruction: Add 600 µl of reagent 1, with RNAse added. Leave the plastic pestle in the tube during the addition of buffer to ensure tissue stuck to the pestle is not lost, mix thoroughly by agitating the pestle. Remove the pestle, taking care to leave as much material inside the tube as possible. Incubate at 37° C for 30 minutes, with occasional mixing by inversion.

steps.title: Add 200 µl of reagent 2...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µl of reagent 2 and mix by inversion until a consistent solution in acquired.

steps.title: (Optional) Add 10 µl proteinase K...
steps.instruction: (Optional) Add 10 µl proteinase K (600U/ml), incubate at 55° C for 2 hours with occasional mixing by inversion.

steps.title: Alternatively, if not using proteinase K,...
steps.instruction: Alternatively, if not using proteinase K, incubate at 65° C for 10 minutes with regular mixing by inversion.

steps.title: Place samples on ice for 20 minutes
steps.instruction: Place samples on ice for 20 minutes.

steps.title: Add 500 µl of pre-cooled chloroform...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µl of pre-cooled chloroform at -20° C, and 100 µl of Nucleon PhytoPure DNA extraction resin suspension, mix by gentle shaking and inversion and mix the sample on a rotator disk for 10 minutes on room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge in a bench-top microfuge (1,300 G) for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge in a bench-top microfuge (1,300 G) for 10 minutes. Transfer the upper aqueous phase to a new tube. Take care not to disturb the interface. Be conservative, and leave some of the upper phases behind rather than carrying over chloroform or resin contamination.

steps.title: (Optional) Add the upper phase to 0
steps.instruction: (Optional) Add the upper phase to 0.05 X volumes of 3M Na OAc (pH 5.2)

steps.title: Add 1 volume of pre-cooled isopropanol at -20° C
steps.instruction: Add 1 volume of pre-cooled isopropanol at -20° C. Add one drop at a time. Mix the tube by inversion. Mix by inversion and incubate at -20° C for 15 minutes.

steps.title: Centrifuge in a bench-top microfuge (4,000 G) for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge in a bench-top microfuge (4,000 G) for 10 minutes. Discard the supernatant without disturbing the pellet. Put the Eppendorf top-down on a paper roll to dry the sample further.

steps.title: Wash the pellet in 1ml of freshly made 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Wash the pellet in 1ml of freshly made 70% ethanol. Inverse the tube five times and centrifuge in a bench-top microfuge (4,000 G) for 20 minutes to collect the pellet.

steps.title: Pour off the ethanol without disturbing...
steps.instruction: Pour off the ethanol without disturbing the pellet and dry the sample by putting the Eppendorf top-down on a paper roll.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 100 μl water, or TE
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 100 μl water, or TE.

steps.title: Asses the molecular weight of the...
steps.instruction: Asses the molecular weight of the DNA sample by checking the DNA fragment sizes on the Agilent FEMTO-pulse Fragment Analyzer, or by PFGE.
